beginner.question: What does justify-content do in Flexbox?
beginner.answer: justify-content aligns flex items along the main axis, distributing space between or around items.

beginner.question: What are the values of justify-content?
beginner.answer: The values are flex-start, flex-end, center, space-between, space-around, space-evenly.

beginner.question: What does justify-content: flex-start do?
beginner.answer: flex-start aligns items at the start of the main axis (left by default in row direction).

beginner.question: What does justify-content: flex-end do?
beginner.answer: flex-end aligns items at the end of the main axis (right by default in row direction).

beginner.question: What does justify-content: center do?
beginner.answer: center aligns items in the center of the main axis.

beginner.question: What does justify-content: space-between do?
beginner.answer: space-between distributes items evenly, with the first item at the start and the last item at the end.

beginner.question: What does justify-content: space-around do?
beginner.answer: space-around distributes items with equal space around each item, but the edge spaces are half the size.

beginner.question: What does justify-content: space-evenly do?
beginner.answer: space-evenly distributes items with equal space around each item, including equal space at the edges.

beginner.question: What is the default value of justify-content?
Does justify-content work on the cross axis?
beginner.answer: No, justify-content always aligns along the main axis; use align-items or align-content for the cross axis.

intermediate.question: How does justify-content interact with flex-direction: column?
intermediate.answer: In column direction, justify-content distributes space vertically along the column axis.

intermediate.question: What happens when justify-content is used with flex-wrap: wrap?
intermediate.answer: justify-content distributes items within each flex line; it does not affect spacing between wrapped lines.

intermediate.question: How does justify-content: space-between differ from space-around?
intermediate.answer: space-between puts first and last items at edges; space-around gives half-size space at edges.

intermediate.question: How does justify-content: space-evenly differ from space-around?
intermediate.answer: space-evenly gives equal space at both edges and between items; space-around gives half spaces at edges.

intermediate.question: Does justify-content work when there is no free space?
intermediate.answer: No, justify-content only has an effect when there is extra space to distribute after items are laid out.

intermediate.question: How does justify-content interact with margins on flex items?
intermediate.answer: If a flex item has margin: auto, it absorbs free space and overrides justify-content alignment.

intermediate.question: How does justify-content affect the start edge in RTL layouts?
intermediate.answer: In RTL, flex-start aligns to the right and flex-end aligns to the left.

intermediate.question: How does justify-content work with absolutely positioned flex items?
intermediate.answer: Absolutely positioned children are not flex items, so justify-content does not affect them.

intermediate.question: How do you center items in the middle of a flex container?
intermediate.answer: Use justify-content: center to center items along the main axis.

intermediate.question: What is the visual difference between space-around and space-evenly?
intermediate.answer: space-around: edge spacing is half the interior spacing; space-evenly: all spacing (edge and interior) is equal.

advanced.question: How does justify-content interact with the gap property?
advanced.answer: gap adds fixed spacing between items on top of what justify-content distributes; justify-content only distributes remaining free space.

advanced.question: How does justify-content: flex-end affect the order property?
advanced.answer: flex-end places items at the end regardless of their order value; items with higher order values appear further from the end.

advanced.question: How does justify-content behave in a multi-line flex container when lines have different widths?
advanced.answer: Each flex line is justified independently, so items in a shorter line will have different spacing than a full line.

advanced.question: What is the difference between justify-content and justify-items in Grid vs Flexbox?
advanced.answer: Justify-content exists in both; however, Flexbox lacks justify-items/justify-self because the main-axis has only one item per line.

advanced.question: How does justify-content: center interact with overflow?
advanced.answer: If items overflow the container with center, items at both ends are clipped equally; the center remains centered.

advanced.question: How does the safe keyword modify justify-content behavior?
advanced.answer: safe ensures items never overflow the container by falling back to flex-start if center would cause overflow.

advanced.question: How does justify-content: space-between behave with a single item?
advanced.answer: With one item, space-between has no effect (no space to distribute), and the item stays at flex-start.

advanced.question: How does justify-content work with different writing modes?
advanced.answer: In vertical writing modes, flex-direction: row follows the vertical inline axis, so justify-content distributes vertically.

advanced.question: How does justify-content distribute space when items have different flex-grow values?
advanced.answer: justify-content distributes free space before flex-grow is applied; growing items may modify the distribution.

advanced.question: How does the CSS Box Alignment spec unify justify-content across Flexbox and Grid?
advanced.answer: Both use the same justify-content property with identical values, following the Box Alignment specification for consistency.
